channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Geopolitical news headlines curated by Frontion

item.title: Microsoft Cuts Hundreds of Cloud Jobs in China as Data Laws Tighten
item.description: Microsoft is laying off employees at its China cloud unit amid ongoing restrictions on cross-border data flows between Beijing and Washington, reflecting growing tech tensions.

item.title: Xi Concludes North Korea Visit, Seeks to Counter Russia Influence
item.description: Chinese President Xi Jinping wrapped up a two-day visit to North Korea aimed at shoring up Beijing's influence in Pyongyang amid concerns about deepening North Korea-Russia ties.

item.title: Iran Says US Revoked World Cup Ticket Allocation for Supporters
item.description: Iran's football federation says the US has withdrawn its World Cup ticket allocation, leaving the nation unable to provide tickets to supporters for the tournament.

item.title: China Firmly Opposes US Blacklisting of Alibaba, Baidu and Other Firms
item.description: Beijing condemned Washington's decision to blacklist major Chinese companies including Alibaba and Baidu, vowing to defend its interests.

item.title: Pakistan and Lebanon Army Chiefs Meet as Regional Mediation Continues
item.description: The heads of the Pakistani and Lebanese armed forces agreed to boost cooperation during talks in Pakistan as Middle East diplomatic efforts drag on.

item.title: Global Conflicts Reach Highest Level Since World War II
item.description: A new report shows global conflicts surged in 2025, reaching levels not seen since World War II, as simultaneous wars in Ukraine, Middle East, and other regions strain international institutions.

item.title: Saudi Contractor Scraps $800 Million IPO Citing Iran War Uncertainty
item.description: Saudi Arabia's Mutlaq Al-Ghowairi Contracting Company cancelled its planned IPO in Riyadh that could have raised up to $800 million, marking one of the first regional offerings shelved since the start of the Iran war.

item.title: Ukraine Recaptures Over 600 Square Kilometers in 2026
item.description: Ukraine's military chief says Kyiv has retaken more than 600 square kilometers of territory in 2026, with independent battlefield mappers confirming Russia's advances have slowed or reversed in recent months.

item.title: Taiwan simulates destroying Chinese invasion force in coastal drill
item.description: Taiwan's military conducted a live-fire exercise firing rockets and artillery to repel an amphibious assault, describing it as a more realistic combat scenario with reduced preparation time amid heightened tensions with Beijing.

item.title: Kenyan police fire tear gas at protesters opposing US Ebola quarantine center
item.description: Police in Nanyuki scattered demonstrators opposing a US-built Ebola quarantine facility for Americans, which Kenyan courts had ordered to halt. The facility was rapidly constructed amid Congo's growing outbreak.

item.title: Congo Ebola outbreak reaches 100 deaths from 550 cases as conflict hampers response
item.description: At least 100 people have died from Ebola in eastern Congo since the outbreak was declared on May 15, with 550 confirmed cases. Armed conflict, attacks on health workers, and lack of approved vaccine or treatment are complicating containment efforts.

item.title: France and Germany scrap $100 billion joint fighter jet program
item.description: The leaders of Germany and France have agreed to abandon the FCAS sixth-generation fighter jet project after years of industrial disputes over Europe's most ambitious defense program, marking a significant setback for European military cooperation.

item.title: US leads record global surge in nuclear weapons spending
item.description: The United States spent $69.2 billion on nuclear arsenals in 2025, more than all other nuclear states combined and representing a 22% increase, according to a campaign group report. Global nuclear spending hit a record high as major powers modernize their deterrents.

item.title: France and Germany Halt €100B FCAS Fighter Jet Program
item.description: Long-running disputes over technology sharing, industrial workshare and intellectual property rights have forced France and Germany to scrap their landmark next-generation fighter jet project, dealing a major blow to European defense ambitions.

item.title: Israel Orders Evacuation of Lebanons Tyre Including Christian Quarter
item.description: The Israeli military issued an evacuation order for the Lebanese city of Tyre, including its Christian quarter, as hostilities with Hezbollah continue amid broader regional tensions.

item.title: Chinas Exports Surge 19.4% Year-on-Year Despite War Conditions
item.description: Chinas exports posted a 19.4% year-on-year increase in May, defying expectations amid severe effects from ongoing conflict in the Middle East and global supply chain disruptions.

item.title: Russian Drone and Missile Strikes Kill 3 Injure 18 in Kharkiv Oblast
item.description: Russian forces launched 11 drone strikes on Kharkiv, with additional missile attacks killing 3 and injuring 18 as Ukraine continues to face sustained aerial bombardment.

item.title: Pentagon adds Alibaba, BYD, Baidu to Chinese military companies list
item.description: The US Defense Department added major Chinese tech firms including Alibaba, Baidu, and BYD to a list of companies believed to aid China's military, escalating tensions with Beijing and warning American businesses about potential risks.

item.title: US judge strikes down Trump's $100,000 H-1B visa fee as unlawful
item.description: A federal judge ruled that the Trump administration's $100,000 fee on new H-1B visas for skilled foreign workers was an unlawful tax not authorized by Congress, blocking a policy that tech companies had opposed.

item.title: Xi Jinping visits North Korea for first time since 2019
item.description: Chinese President Xi Jinping met Kim Jong Un in Pyongyang in a summit aimed at strengthening bilateral ties, as North Korea has grown closer to Russia amid the Ukraine war.

item.title: Philippines earthquake death toll rises as rescuers search rubble
item.description: A powerful 7.8-magnitude earthquake struck the southern Philippines, killing at least 37 people and injuring hundreds. Rescuers continued searching collapsed buildings for survivors amid ongoing aftershocks.

item.title: Somali World Cup referee denied entry to US days before tournament
item.description: FIFA referee Omar Abdulkadir Artan was denied entry to the United States just days before the 2026 World Cup, ending his historic opportunity to become the first Somali to officiate at the tournament.

item.title: US Army Apache Helicopter Crashes Near Strait of Hormuz, Crew Rescued
item.description: A US Army Apache gunship went down near the strategic Strait of Hormuz waterway. President Trump confirmed both pilots are safe, though the cause remains unclear amid ongoing tensions with Iran in the region.

item.title: Ebola Death Toll in DR Congo Climbs to 101
item.description: The Democratic Republic of Congo is fighting its 17th Ebola outbreak since the virus was first identified in 1976, with the death toll now exceeding 100 as health authorities struggle to contain the spread.

item.title: Investors See Tipping Point for Bonds After Geopolitics Shreds Old Playbooks
item.description: Bonds have failed to shield investors during the Iran-Israel conflict, but some fund managers believe debt markets may soon reclaim their safe-haven role if inflation begins to impact economic growth.
